rationale: Both correctly separate timely feasibility, retrospective certification, benchmark limits, and unsupported operational outcomes. B is more usable for managers: its table cleanly distinguishes what was established at 10 versus 35 minutes, and it more precisely limits the benchmark claim to the tested instance and setup. A is substantively sound but slightly muddies incumbent quality with contemporaneous knowledge and refers to tested instances in the plural without support. Both are somewhat repetitive, so prose economy is tied.
